title: Bug fixes (3) + conformal prediction intervals for crash probabilities

bugs_found: economic_surprise.py: _fetch_fred_series hardcoded observation_start='2020-01-01' despite _LOOKBACK_YEARS=3 constant — trend window includes increasingly stale data as time passes, diluting the surprise signal quality

stress_testing.py: _estimate_crisis_return uses identical sector multipliers for ALL scenarios — GFC should penalize Financials 1.8x not 1.4x, Dot-Com should penalize Tech 2.0x not 1.3x, COVID Tech should be 0.6x not 1.3x. Systematic estimation error for stocks without historical data

attribution.py: full_portfolio_analytics builds benchmark as {SPY: 1.0} — a single-asset benchmark makes Brinson-Fachler sector-level allocation effects identically zero, defeating the purpose of BF attribution
observation.gap_identified: Crash model provides point estimates with no uncertainty quantification — users cannot distinguish 'high-confidence 7%' from 'uncertain 7% that could be 2%-20%'. Conformal prediction (Vovk 2005) adds calibrated intervals with finite-sample coverage guarantees.

bugs_fixed: economic_surprise.py: Dynamic observation_start computed from _LOOKBACK_YEARS + 1 year buffer, replacing hardcoded '2020-01-01'

stress_testing.py: Added scenario-specific sector multiplier tables for all 6 scenarios (GFC, COVID, Dot-Com, Black Monday, Rate Shock, Volmageddon) with empirically-calibrated values reflecting actual sector performance in each crisis

attribution.py: Replaced trivial {SPY: 1.0} benchmark with proper sector-level benchmark weights approximating S&P 500 composition. Added _build_sector_benchmark() that distributes benchmark weight across sector representative tickers from config
implementation.feature_built: Conformal prediction intervals for crash probabilities — split conformal prediction (Vovk, Gammerman & Shafer, 2005) that wraps the LightGBM+LR crash model with calibrated uncertainty bands. Includes: ConformalCrashPredictor class with calibrate/predict/save/load, heuristic fallback for uncalibrated scenarios, monotonicity enforcement across horizons, integration into crash prediction API endpoint, integration into model training pipeline (auto-saves conformal_scores.pkl), new /api/analytics/conformal-interval endpoint

limitations: Conformal scores require model retraining to populate — until then, heuristic fallback is used (beta-distribution-inspired widths, no coverage guarantee)
Attribution sector benchmark uses approximate S&P 500 weights that may drift from actual composition; ideally would use live sector ETF weights
Scenario-specific sector multipliers are empirically estimated from historical data, not dynamically computed from actual crisis returns
assessment.self_critique: Could have also integrated conformal intervals into the stock-level crash display (stock_analyzer.py) for per-ticker uncertainty bands. The sector benchmark weights in attribution are static approximations — a more robust approach would fetch actual sector ETF weights (XLK, XLF, XLE etc.) at runtime. Would do that in a future cycle.

next_steps: Integrate conformal intervals into stock_analyzer.py per-ticker crash display
Add volatility surface interpolation for options intelligence
Wire VIX term structure into regime detection for faster regime transitions
Add conformal interval display to frontend crash prediction page
Improve attribution benchmark with live sector ETF weights
